reasoning: The informal statement requires $G_1$ and $G_2$ to be cyclic groups of (finite) orders $m$ and $n$; the Lean signature captures finiteness via `[Fintype G] [Fintype H]` and cyclicity via `IsCyclic G`, `IsCyclic H`. The conclusion `IsCyclic (G × H) ↔ (card G).Coprime (card H)` correctly encodes "G × H is cyclic iff m and n are relatively prime": `Fintype.card` gives the group order and `Nat.Coprime` is `Nat.gcd = 1`, which is the standard definition of relatively prime. Hypotheses, types, quantifier structure (universally quantified over any such G and H), and conclusion all match the informal claim with no added or dropped content.
